title: argyranthemum callichrysum subsp. gomerensis
intro: Argyranthemum callichrysum subsp. gomerensis, also known as Gomeran daisy or Gomeran marguerite, is a flowering plant in the daisy family. It is native to the Canary Islands, where it is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic effects. The plant is valued for its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems, offering relief from coughs and colds. Argyranthemum callichrysum subsp. gomerensis can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, allowing for various herbal preparations to harness its health benefits.
